premise: I spent $110,000 of my pension to buy a fourplex for my son and his wife, trusting them to manage the property and send me the rental income for my retirement, but they forged a gift deed, sold the building for $320,000, and bought a waterfront condo in cash. I proved the forgery, won a $350,000 fraud judgment, and when they couldn’t pay, I seized and auctioned the condo, bought it myself, and now rent it back to them at full market rate while I live in the penthouse — every month their rent check reminds them whose name is on the deed.
